Home / Project Management & Kanban / Alternatives to Basecamp

3 open source alternatives to Basecamp

Flat-fee project management and team coordination. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Basecamp

  • Opinionated workflow — not for every team.
  • No native Gantt or sprint features.
  • Cloud-only.

Current Basecamp pricing (for reference): Basecamp Pro Unlimited $299/month for unlimited users.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Kanboard
Minimalist PHP-based kanban project manager. 		Minimalist project boards without Basecamp's opinionated flows. MIT ★☆☆☆☆ Self-host only
Redmine
Classic Rails-based issue tracker used for decades. 		Teams wanting a mature, long-established alternative. GPL-2.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
OpenProject
Enterprise-ready open source project management. 		When you outgrow Basecamp and need Gantt and budgets. GPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes

1. Kanboard — Minimalist project boards without Basecamp's opinionated flows.

Minimalist PHP-based kanban project manager.

Strengths

  • Runs on plain LAMP — no containers needed.
  • Minimal, focused kanban experience.
  • Stable, long-running project.

Weaknesses

  • UI is utilitarian, not fancy.
  • Feature set narrower than Planka or Focalboard.
  • No native mobile app.
License: MIT Self-host difficulty: 1/5

Kanboard homepage · Source on GitHub · Basecamp vs Kanboard →

2. Redmine — Teams wanting a mature, long-established alternative.

Classic Rails-based issue tracker used for decades.

Strengths

  • Very mature — rock solid reliability.
  • Plugin ecosystem for almost anything.
  • Multi-project with roles and workflows.

Weaknesses

  • UI feels dated.
  • Rails deployment is heavier than Go/Node alternatives.
  • Community is slower than modern competitors.
License: GPL-2.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud optionMobile: iOS, Android

Redmine homepage · Source on GitHub · Basecamp vs Redmine →

3. OpenProject — When you outgrow Basecamp and need Gantt and budgets.

Enterprise-ready open source project management.

Strengths

  • Strong Gantt, roadmaps and budget features.
  • BIM (Building Information Modeling) for construction industry.
  • Active enterprise adoption.

Weaknesses

  • Community edition lacks some premium features.
  • Setup has more moving parts than lightweight tools.
  • UI density can intimidate newcomers.
License: GPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

OpenProject homepage · Source on GitHub · Basecamp vs OpenProject →
